Leukoplakia 
Leukoplakia is a white patch or patches in the mouth. If you have a white patch in your mouth that does not go away, get it checked by a dentist or GP.
Symptoms of leukoplakia 
Leukoplakia patches:
are white patches in the mouth are not usually painful are an irregular shape may be slightly raised may have red areas within the patch cannot be rubbed or scraped away (patches that can be removed could be oral thrush ) 
Leukoplakia patches often appear on or below the tongue.
